sourcePage.rawText:
14   Consider the following simultaneous equations, where p is a real number:

                                       p2x + log2 y = 2

                                        2x + log2 y = 1


     What is the complete range of p for which these simultaneous equations have a real
     solution (x, y)?

     A p<1

     B p = 1

     C p>1

     D p < 1 or p > 2

     E p = 1 and p < 2

     F p > 1 and p < 2

     G p>2

     H All real values of p




                                            16

stem.rawText:
Consider the following simultaneous equations, where p is a real number:

                                       p2x + log2 y = 2

                                        2x + log2 y = 1


     What is the complete range of p for which these simultaneous equations have a real
     solution (x, y)?

solution.rawText:
Writing X = 2x and Y = log2 y gives the simultaneous equations

                                          pX + Y = 2
                                           X +Y =1
                                                                   1
Subtracting gives (p − 1)X = 1, so as long as p ̸= 1, we have X = p−1 and so Y = p−2
                                                                                 p−1 .
                              p−2
Now the equation log2 y = p−1      gives a real value for y for any value of p ̸= 1 (specifically
     (p−2)/(p−1)                            1                                  1
y=2              ), but the equation 2x = p−1   has a solution if and only if p−1 > 0, which is if
and only if p > 1.
Therefore the original simultaneous equations have a real solution (x, y) if and only if p > 1,
which is option C.




Version 1.0, March 2022                                                                   Page 17
